title: From The "Divan." (Translations From The Hebrew Poets Of Medaeval Spain.)
content:
My thoughts impelled me to the resting-place
    Where sleep my parents, many a friend and brother.
    I asked them (no one heard and none replied):
    "Do ye forsake me, too, oh father, mother?"
    Then from the grave, without a tongue, these cried,
    And showed my own place waiting by their side.

Moses Ben Esra (About 1100).
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Emma Lazarus delivers a powerful performance in "From The "Divan." (Translations From The Hebrew Poets Of Medaeval Spain.)"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
